title: ODI Matrix: Continuing Care Retirement Communities (NAICS 623311)

sections.title: Incumbent Technology Context (Satisfaction Recalibration Basis)
sections.content:
Incumbents currently hired in CCRC for elopement prevention / emergency exit security:

| Incumbent | Market Share Est. | Mechanism | Strengths | Weaknesses |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| RFID/BLE Wander-Management (Stanley Healthcare, CenTrak) | ~40% | RFID wristbands trigger door alerts; software-managed lock states | Resident-level tracking, EHR integration (Stanley), customizable alert rules | Requires wristband compliance; loses monitoring during power outages (UPS needed); no direct door physical locking; expensive infrastructure |
| Delayed-Egress EM Locks (dormakaba, ASSA ABLOY, Allegion) | ~35% | Electric magnet holds door for 15 s delay after handle activation; unlocks on fire signal | Physical barrier, CMS-compliant 15 s delay, fire-alarm interlock | Requires mains wiring; fails open during power outage (loses delay + monitoring); expensive retrofit; licensed electrical install |
| Basic mechanical alarm + manual patrol | ~25% | Simple door-open alarm horn; staff inspection rounds | Low cost; power-independent alarm; no infrastructure overhead | No remote monitoring; no CMS documentation; events detected only at patrol intervals (hours) |

**Key recalibration signals:**
- EM locks and RFID systems **lose monitoring during extended power outages** → N07 satisfaction downgraded (6 → 4)
- CMS-formatted automated documentation exists only in premium Stanley deployments → N21 satisfaction downgraded (5 → 4); N18 unchanged (already 3)
- Physical mechanism defeat (guard removed/tampered) is **undetectable by electrical monitoring** → N06 satisfaction downgraded (4 → 3)
- Inovonics + Stanley achieve near-real-time alerting in well-equipped facilities → N22 satisfaction upgraded (4 → 5)

---

sections.title: Step 1 + 2 — Error Statements with Recalibrated Scores
sections.content:
### Market-Level Needs (from JTBD Step 06)

| ID | Error Statement | Job Step | Imp | Sat_init | Sat_recal | Recal Reason |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N07 | Minimize the likelihood of emergency exit alarm silence during an extended power outage (>8 h) on an active Memory Care unit | Execute | 10.0 | 6.0 | 4.0 | EM locks fail open; RFID wander systems lose monitoring without UPS; only basic mechanical alarm is power-independent |
| N21 | Minimize the likelihood of a CMS Immediate Jeopardy citation resulting from an undocumented emergency exit elopement event | Monitor | 10.0 | 5.0 | 4.0 | Real-time CMS-formatted documentation absent in most deployments; Stanley generates post-hoc logs but not automated real-time packages |
| N05 | Minimize the time to detect a non-functional emergency exit guard during active Memory Care hours (<4 h) | Monitor | 9.0 | 3.0 | 3.0 | No recalibration needed; no incumbent proactively detects mechanical failure — patrol-based discovery only |
| N18 | Minimize the likelihood of incomplete emergency exit inspection and event documentation at annual CMS survey | Monitor | 9.0 | 3.0 | 3.0 | No recalibration; manual document preparation remains the norm; Stanley partial coverage only in premium installs |
| N01 | Minimize the likelihood of unmonitored Memory Care emergency exits being identified during a CMS site survey | Plan | 9.0 | 5.0 | 4.0 | EM lock and RFID deployments frequently leave secondary exits uncovered due to retrofit cost; coverage gaps common |
| N10 | Minimize the time between unauthorized emergency exit activation and staff notification during overnight / weekend staffing | Monitor | 9.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | No recalibration; Inovonics achieves reasonable overnight alerting but is not universally deployed |
| N13 | Minimize the likelihood of undetected battery depletion creating a wireless monitoring gap on an emergency exit door | Monitor | 8.0 | 3.0 | 3.0 | No recalibration; basic mechanical alarms have no battery monitoring; Inovonics supervised check-ins are exception, not norm |
| N17 | Minimize the time and effort required to generate emergency exit compliance documentation for CMS regulatory review | Monitor | 8.0 | 3.0 | 3.0 | No recalibration; manual preparation remains dominant; documented automation rare |
| N06 | Minimize the likelihood of the monitoring dashboard showing a guard as armed while the physical mechanism is defeated or removed | Monitor | 8.0 | 4.0 | 3.0 | Physical mechanism defeat is undetectable by electrical monitoring; even bidirectional radio check-ins only detect radio silence, not hardware defeat |
| N22 | Minimize the time between emergency exit guard activation and Memory Care staff notification to less than 30 seconds | Respond | 8.0 | 4.0 | 5.0 | Inovonics + Stanley Healthcare achieve near-real-time mobile alerting in equipped facilities; this need is better served than others |

### Product-Related Needs (from Step 10, job_step = "product_constraint")

| ID | Error Statement | Source Constraint | Imp | Sat_recal |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| PRN-01 | Minimize the likelihood of IoT module battery depletion going undetected on a low-traffic CCRC wing, creating a wireless monitoring gap during overnight staffing | C4 — IoT Battery Life Floor | 8.0 | 3.0 |
| PRN-05 | Minimize the time to discover that a wireless receiver serving multiple door guardians on a single care level has lost connectivity, leaving all exits without event transmission while mechanical alarms remain functional | C3 + C6 | 8.0 | 3.0 |
| PRN-02 | Minimize the likelihood of wireless alarm event transmission failure from emergency exit doors in reinforced concrete corridors or basement wings exceeding the 30 m EasyWave transmitter range | C3 — Wireless Range | 7.0 | 4.0 |
| PRN-04 | Minimize the likelihood of unauthorized guard removal or mechanism defeat by a cognitively impaired resident manipulating the exposed surface-mounted guard housing on the door handle | C5 — Surface Mount | 7.0 | 4.0 |
| PRN-03 | Minimize the time to integrate EasyWave-protocol door guardian event streams into an existing BACnet or KNX building management platform deployed across a continuing care campus | C6 — Protocol Lock-In | 6.0 | 3.0 |

---

sections.title: Step 3 — Opportunity Scores (Deterministic)
sections.content:
`is_underserved = importance > 7.0 AND satisfaction < 5.0`
`is_overserved  = importance < 3.0 AND satisfaction > 7.0`

| ID | Imp | Sat | Opp Score | Underserved | Overserved | Product-Related | Zone |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N07 | 10.0 | 4.0 | **16.00** | ✓ | — | — | Highly Underserved |
| N21 | 10.0 | 4.0 | **16.00** | ✓ | — | — | Highly Underserved |
| N05 | 9.0 | 3.0 | **15.00** | ✓ | — | — | Highly Underserved |
| N18 | 9.0 | 3.0 | **15.00** | ✓ | — | — | Highly Underserved |
| N01 | 9.0 | 4.0 | **14.00** | ✓ | — | — | Highly Underserved |
| N10 | 9.0 | 4.0 | **14.00** | ✓ | — | — | Highly Underserved |
| N13 | 8.0 | 3.0 | **13.00** | ✓ | — | — | Highly Underserved |
| N17 | 8.0 | 3.0 | **13.00** | ✓ | — | — | Highly Underserved |
| N06 | 8.0 | 3.0 | **13.00** | ✓ | — | — | Highly Underserved |
| PRN-01 | 8.0 | 3.0 | **13.00** | ✓ | — | ✓ | Highly Underserved |
| PRN-05 | 8.0 | 3.0 | **13.00** | ✓ | — | ✓ | Highly Underserved |
| N22 | 8.0 | 5.0 | **11.00** | — | — | — | Appropriately Served |
| PRN-02 | 7.0 | 4.0 | **10.00** | — | — | ✓ | Appropriately Served |
| PRN-04 | 7.0 | 4.0 | **10.00** | — | — | ✓ | Appropriately Served |
| PRN-03 | 6.0 | 3.0 | **9.00** | — | — | ✓ | Appropriately Served |

**Formula verification (spot-check):**
- N07: 10.0 + max(10.0−4.0, 0) = 10.0 + 6.0 = 16.00 ✓
- N22: 8.0 + max(8.0−5.0, 0) = 8.0 + 3.0 = 11.00 ✓; is_underserved = (8.0>7 AND 5.0<5) = false ✓
- PRN-03: 6.0 + max(6.0−3.0, 0) = 6.0 + 3.0 = 9.00 ✓

---

sections.title: Step 5 — Cross-Check: ODI vs Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
Feature-Market Fit averages from FIT_continuing_care_retirement.md (Step 09):

| Feature | Kano | Overall Fit |
|---|---|---|
| Mechanical Handle Locking | must_be | 6.00 |
| Audible Alarm Trigger | must_be | 6.22 |
| Single-Motion Emergency Release | must_be | 5.89 |
| Key Override Bypass | one_dimensional | 5.89 |
| Battery-Powered Operation | attractive | 6.44 |
| Pre-Alarm Warning | one_dimensional | 4.67 |
| **Average** | | **5.85** |

| Threshold | Condition | Result |
|---|---|---|
| HIGH opportunity + LOW fit | ODI avg > 12 AND Fit avg < 4 | **Not triggered** (ODI 13.0 > 12 ✓, but Fit 5.85 > 4) |
| LOW opportunity + HIGH fit | ODI avg < 8 AND Fit avg > 7 | Not triggered |

**Interpretation:** No inconsistency flag. CCRC is a high-opportunity market structurally underserved by all incumbent solutions (ODI avg 13.0). Technology-class fit is moderate (5.85/10) — the product class addresses the must-be requirements (physical locking, audible alarm, single-motion release) but has structural gaps on monitoring depth (N05, N06), documentation automation (N17, N18), and workflow integration. The gap signals addressable product-extension opportunity rather than product-market mismatch.

---

sections.title: Key Findings
sections.content:
| Finding | Detail |
|---|---|
| 11 of 15 needs are Highly Underserved | All of importance ≥ 8 with satisfaction ≤ 4 qualify — incumbents systematically fail this market |
| Zero overserved needs | No feature is over-delivered; no cost-reduction-only play available |
| Documentation gap is the #2 and #4 opportunity | N21 (CMS IJ citation) + N18 (survey completeness) — both at opp ≥ 15 — are pure software/workflow gaps, not hardware gaps |
| Power-independence is the top mechanical gap | N07 (opp=16) is the highest-scoring need; Türwächter's mechanical primary alarm directly addresses it; EM lock incumbents do not |
| Product-related needs (PRN-01, PRN-05) are also highly underserved | Battery monitoring and receiver connectivity detection are structural IoT architecture gaps shared with most wireless alternatives |
| N22 (alert latency) is the only appropriately-served market need | Inovonics + Stanley mobile alerting adequately addresses this; not a differentiation lever |

---

Minimize the time to integrate EasyWave-protocol door guardian event streams into an existing BACnet or KNX building management platform deployed across a continuing care campus

top_opportunities: Minimize the likelihood of emergency exit alarm silence during an extended power outage (>8 h) on an active Memory Care unit
Minimize the likelihood of a CMS Immediate Jeopardy citation resulting from an undocumented emergency exit elopement event
Minimize the time to detect a non-functional emergency exit guard during active Memory Care hours (target <4 h)
Minimize the likelihood of incomplete emergency exit inspection and event documentation at annual CMS survey

cross_check.note: No inconsistency. ODI avg 13.0 confirms high structural underservice. Feature-Fit avg 5.85 is above the 4.0 alert threshold — technology class is relevant but has capability gaps on monitoring depth, documentation automation, and workflow integration.
